description: kinSoftChallenge kinetic-rate validation reference (PRD §8 NFR-VALID(c), §9 M8). Ground-truth kinetic models and the reported inter-tool spread of the blind benchmark, plus Tether's own measured deviation. The oracle checks that Tether's fitted rates on a level fall within that dataset's reported inter-tool spread — an ADVISORY check (never gates main).

source.citation: Götz M, Barth A, Bohr SS-R, et al. A blind benchmark of analysis tools to infer kinetic rate constants from single-molecule FRET trajectories. Nature Communications 13:5402 (2022).
source.ground_truth_source: Supplementary Table 5 (simulation parameters, kinetic-model diagrams) and Supplementary Table 1 (Fig. 4 ground-truth rate matrix) of the Supplementary Information; rate constants in s^-1.
source.spread_source: Main text: 2-state (Fig. 2) inferred rate constants across the 14 analyses deviate at most 12% from ground truth (5% average); FRET efficiencies within 17% (1% average). Finite-dataset MLE uncertainty floor is >=3% (1 sigma). The 3-state (Fig. 3) best tools reach 9-14% average deviation; the 4-state (Fig. 4) case is compared via cumulative dwell-time distributions, not direct rates.

band.rationale: The reported inter-tool spread for the archetypal 2-state simulated dataset: the 14 benchmark analyses inferred rate constants within a maximum of 12% of ground truth (5% average), above a >=3% (1 sigma) finite-dataset floor. Used as the advisory acceptance band on |k - GT|/GT per rate constant. Tether's own base-env fit lands at 2.3%/3.1% (see levels.level1.tether_measured), ~4x inside the band.

tether_measured.method: base-env 2-state Gaussian HMM (Baum-Welch + Viterbi) + pooled dwell-time MLE (k = 1/<tau>)
tether_measured.note: Measured on the committed kinsoft_sim.hdf5 level1 (75 traces); both rates ~4x inside the 12% band. See tests/test_kinsoft_kinetics.py.

level2.deferred_reason: A directional non-equilibrium 3-state system with per-trace emission/excitation variability; the benchmark's best tools reach only 9-14% average GT deviation and most systematically mis-allocate 1<->3 transitions. A faithful oracle needs a 3-state idealizer plus net-flow handling; recorded here for a future extension.

ground_truth.note: The 1<->2 (low<->mid) rates are read exactly from the Suppl. Table 5 diagram; the four state-3 (high-FRET) arm magnitudes are read from the diagram but their exact source->dest assignment (and the counter-clockwise flow direction) must be taken from the Zenodo simulation config before activating an oracle.

level3.system: 4-state kinetic heterogeneity (two low-FRET + two high-FRET states; donor/acceptor blinking)
level3.deferred_reason: States 1,2 share the low-FRET level and states 3,4 share the high-FRET level, so a FRET-only idealizer sees only 2 states and cannot resolve the 4-state kinetics. The benchmark itself compares this case via cumulative dwell-time distributions, not direct rate constants. Recorded exactly (Suppl. Table 1) for a future dwell-CDF extension.

ground_truth.note: Full 4-state ground-truth rate matrix from Supplementary Table 1 (all other off-diagonal rates are 0); blinking kbright=7 s^-1, kdark=0.007 s^-1.
